meta.title: Jules Rook Conversation
meta.description: Jules Rook confrontation in the main room. Old history, old wounds.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Funny. You always did arrive once the room was already built.

choices.text: I built what I could with what I had.
I did. I did. I was drowning and I picked the stupidest possible version of air.
You saying this to help me or because the room finally came with witnesses?

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Built. Right. You built like a man pulling planks from someone else's house and calling it architecture.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Good luck tonight. I mean that in the way people mean it when they've stopped caring about the outcome.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Well. That's new. You admitting fault without a backing track.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: I'm not forgiving you. But I'm not here to collect, either. Do something worth the room tonight. That's all I've got.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Both. Obviously both. You think honesty and performance are different things? They haven't been since we were seventeen.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Fine. You've still got the instinct. Whether you deserve it is between you and whatever you're calling a conscience these days.

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Nothing? Really? You're going to stand there and let that land?

nodes.speaker: jules_rook
nodes.text: Alright. Maybe that is the answer. Maybe you finally ran out of clever. There are worse things to lose.
